source.rationale: All prior cases (001, 002, 006) are parameter/feature ADDITIONS. This tests a qualitatively different change_type: a parameter and its entire feature (caching) REMOVED from the function. Checks two things the addition-only cases can't: does the Verifier correctly flag staleness caused by removal (not just omission of something new), and does the Corrector correctly propose deleting the now-nonexistent `use_cache` mention rather than leaving it in place or inventing a justification for why it might still work.

stale_doc_section: Call `get_user_profile(user_id, use_cache=True)` to retrieve a user's profile. Set `use_cache=False` to bypass the cache and always fetch fresh data from the database.
human_fixed_doc_section: Call `get_user_profile(user_id)` to retrieve a user's profile. The profile is always fetched directly from the database; caching was removed.

labels.notes_for_labeler: DECIDED (human review): same call as case 005, reached after checking the actual proposed text (which does fully remove the stale use_cache mention — confirmed this wasn't a real leftover-content miss) and cross-referencing case 001's real human fix, which also doesn't narrate that a parameter is new. Judged a Validator miscalibration, not desired behavior. validator_status intentionally left unscored (key omitted), same as case 005, pending a possible future prompt revision.
